programmes.show_description: A look at how products are manufactured around the world. How are Russian dolls manufactured, how is aloe vera produced and how are flags made? S10, Ep17
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. How is vodka produced, how are Chinamax ships manufactured and how is abalone made? S10, Ep18
VW T2 Panel Van. Mike hunts out a classic camper but impossibly high prices force him to settle for a less popular panel model. Can Edd realise the ambitious plans for its upgrade? S7, Ep3
BMW 840. It sounds like the ultimate 90s supercar bargain and Mike couldn't be more pleased with his V8, but when he sees the state of it, Edd doesn't feel as enthusiastic. S7, Ep4
Steam, Bicycles And Metal Rolling. In Cornwall, Henry and Sam find some very rare early-1900s steam-powered cars. If they're very lucky, the duo might even get to ride in one! S2, Ep14
Signs And Wonders. In Somerset, Henry and Sam visit a man with an amazing collection of motorbikes. He has some rare finds, but are they too expensive for the guys? S2, Ep15
Follow the production process of tumble dryers, bubblegum and fireworks, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep10
Follow the production process of artificial eyes, dog and cat food, and mirrors, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep11
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Will cleans out a table that has been damaged by insects and Brenton restores a bronze statue of a beautiful femme fatale. Dom works on a child's pedal car. S4, Ep17
Different Is Good. Drew and Alister are on the hunt for exciting stock. They visit a business specialising in the creation of artificial flowers for film and theatre. S18, Ep11
Treading New Ground. Treading new ground across the UK, Drew and Viki explore the factory complex of a historic British cloth producer and a sprawling reclamation yard. S18, Ep12
The Mahoneys look for gold in one of the world's most remote regions. Meanwhile, the Victorian Diggers may have stumbled across millions of dollars' worth of gold. S4, Ep8
The Victoria Diggers might have found a multi-million-dollar load, but they need help extracting it. The Scrappers continue to struggle with broken machines. S4, Ep9
A very old clock ticks for Tim at Salisbury Cathedral on the last leg of his trip with Irita. She's currently out in front but will he overtake her? S22, Ep10
Angus and the team search for interesting items at a historic home in Coventry. Later, they travel to Aberdeenshire to collect some unusual Asian furniture. S1, Ep10
Will cleans out a table that has been damaged by insects and Brenton restores a bronze statue of a beautiful femme fatale. Dom works on a child's pedal car. S4, Ep17
Riding Shotgun. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk, checking out a collection of vintage bikes. Henry has his eye on petrol pumps, but Sam has other ideas after seeing the sidecars. S2, Ep16
Spoilt For Choice. In Lowestoft, Henry and Sam can't believe their luck when they check out Charlie's wares. He has everything from cars and bikes to stationary engines. S2, Ep17
Paul Webb. Henry and Fuzz visit Paul in the Malvern Hills, a man with a perfectly maintained collection of Japanese motorbikes including a Honda 750 Four. S6, Ep19
Frank. Henry and Fuzz are in Moretonhampstead at Frank's motoring museum - home to hundreds of cars, bikes and rare pieces of motoring memorabilia. S6, Ep20
Anneka Rice & Antony Cotton. Angus clears the family home of TV icon Anneka Rice and looks at a collection of quirky items at soap star Antony Cotton's Lancashire cottage. S2, Ep1
In search of treasure, Angus visits the stunning home of former royal correspondent Jennie Bond and clears soap legend Sid Owen's idyllic French retreat. S2, Ep2
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep14
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep15
Chocks Away! Angus helps a widow in Lincoln find new homes for her late husband's aviation paintings. Plus, he finds a ring with some serious bling in Essex. S6, Ep1
Musical instrument expert Pete transforms a 90-year-old child's drum kit. Meanwhile, Steve reignites an old oil lamp that belonged to Christine's grandfather. S5, Ep39
Music expert Pete puts his skills to the test on a cherished brass instrument that's lost its lustre. Meanwhile, Dominic restores an old-school roundabout. S5, Ep40
A look at how products are made around the world. Featuring the St Petersburg metro, the world's most expensive coffee and chip makers. S10, Ep19
A look at how products are made. Featuring how leather is produced, how goods are moved down the Grand Canal and how Flyboard is created. S10, Ep20
How do they make the king of cheeses, Parmesan? How do they keep the runways running at the world's busiest airports? How do they manufacture relaxing reclining chairs? S11, Ep1
How do they weave a Turkish carpet? How do they use helicopter blade technology for cross country skis, and control the water at Niagara Falls? S11, Ep2
Triumph Stag. Mike tries to find a rust-free classic Stag, but it's harder than he thinks. Ed will have to keep costs super low for any chance of a profit on this one. S7, Ep5
Lotus Elan. The boys are challenged to find a Lotus Elan that hasn't been snapped up by restorers. Once they've tracked one down, Mike falls in love after minutes behind the wheel. S7, Ep6
Riding Shotgun. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk, checking out a collection of vintage bikes. Henry has his eye on petrol pumps, but Sam has other ideas after seeing the sidecars. S2, Ep16
Spoilt For Choice. In Lowestoft, Henry and Sam can't believe their luck when they check out Charlie's wares. He has everything from cars and bikes to stationary engines. S2, Ep17
Follow the production process of fresh cut flowers, adhesive tape and tofu, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep12
Follow the production process of filing cabinets and blown glass, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep13
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Invitation Only. Wayne Carini welcomes viewers into high-end car collecting. He presents an exclusive look at the hottest parties occurring at the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance. S6, Ep17
Cannonball's Run. Wayne is hired to sell automotive journalist Brock Yates' Eliminator roadster; the vehicle is picked up from the Saratoga Automobile Museum and taken to auction. S6, Ep18
Back to the Beach. Derek Hill, the son of legendary auto racer Phil Hill, brings his father's 1912 Packard to the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance. S6, Ep19
Backyard Finds. Wayne hunts for more high-end beauties by exploring old stomping grounds, where he uncovers numerous Mopar cars, a 1905 Cadillac and a Ferrari 330. S6, Ep20
Alfa Romeo Alfasud. Mike takes a risk by buying a late Alfasud 1.5 TI at auction. With no service history, dodgy brakes, and clapped out suspension, can Edd work his magic? S12, Ep12
1963 Volvo PV544. Mike was challenged to find a classic endurance sports car and the Volvo PV544 fit the bill. Mike finds a running example, but with an engine that few can master. S13, Ep2
Saab 9-3. Mike wants a high performance car with an historic badge, but it's hard to find a Saab 9-3 Turbo at a good price. With tight margins will Edd have any room for manoeuvre? S8, Ep5
Renault Alpine. Mike is in France to hunt down the rare Renault Alpine 310. The car he finds is great, but it has some surprises in store, and Edd is charged with getting it fixed. S9, Ep4
Karmann Ghia. Mike is in Idaho to buy the ultimate retro sportster, the Karmann Ghia. Edd loves it, but will the broken gearbox and shoddy interior stop them making a profit? S8, Ep9
2000 Porsche 996. Mike is excited to pick up one of his favourite types of convertible: the 2000 Porsche 996. With only a few issues, this car will be an absolute steal for him. S15, Ep10
2004 BMW M3. Mike finds a BMW six-cylinder modern classic, the E46 M3, with the subframe already fixed. He challenges Ant to transform the transmission with new tech. S15, Ep19
Triumph Spitfire. In Gloucestershire, Henry and Fuzz take on a risky Triumph Spitfire that has been in a crash. In their biggest restoration challenge yet, can they fix it? S1, Ep4
Henry tackles his biggest project, restoring a Bell 47 helicopter for the 80th Anniversary Flight. It's a race against time to get it off the ground.
Old Mates And Motor-Racing. Richard gets into hot water as he tries to upgrade his racing licence and the Cogs head to the Isle of Man TT to help launch a new British motorbike. S5, Ep6
MotoGP: Brazil Sprint Race. All the action from the second Sprint Race of the 2026 MotoGP World Championship, at the Autódromo Internacional de Goiânia in Brazil. S1, Ep2
Henry Cole and the team take on a classic 1950s Massey Ferguson combine harvester in one of their biggest restorations yet.
The expert restorers breathe new life into beloved family heirlooms: a mug with a pottery frog inside, a model windmill and a marching bass drum. S8, Ep11
Jugtastic! Angus visits a home with a pub in it, filled with over 160 jugs. Later, he encounters a colossal collection of Glen Millar Vinyl and V-discs! S6, Ep2
Last Chance Dig. The Blacklighters have just one week left to strike opal before the summer heat shuts them down. The Bushmen scramble to beat mechanical failures. S8, Ep19
